titleStmt.title: Letter to Colonel F.A. Heygate Lambert, 20 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter to Colonel F.A. Heygate Lambert regarding the release of 152 Irish prisoners.
In 1916 Col. F.A. Heygate Lambert (1857-1929) was serving as the Commandant of Frongoch Internment Camp. Lambert served in the camp until the last of the prisoners were released in December 1916. In the Spring of 1917 he went to Ypres to command a battalion of the Labour Corps.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
313106 20th July 1916. Sir, I am directed by the Secretary of State to inform
you that, on the recommendatio of the Advisry Committee,
he authorises the release from internment of the 152 persons
names in the accompsnying lists, this being the fourth
group of prisoners whose release he has authorised. These releases should be carried out in accordance
with the instructions already sent you. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, The Commandant,
Prisoners of War Camp,
Prongoah.
nr. Bala,
N. Wales
LIMERICK CASES EXEMPT.
945 McINERNEY, Thos. A.
452 QUIGELY, Jas.
947 RUTTLE, Samuel.
CORK 2 EXEMPT.
1463 BUCKLEY, Wm. Banteer
552 O'CALLAGHAN, Corn. Mill Road
1104 CASEY, Wm. Mitchellstown
1104 ROCHE, Pat. F. "
